name: 黃如瑩
objective:
Objective:
The purpose of this course is, first, to clarify key concepts in the understanding and interpretation of literary/cultural texts, and secondly, to demonstrate the ways in which important steps and maneuvers should be undertaken in order to compose a piece of promising academic writing. All the research and academic writing in this field follows MLA Style, which then becomes a focus of study and practice in this course.
schedule:
Research Methodology for the Cultural and Literary Studies
With Prof. Ju-ying Vinia Huang 
E-mail: vinia@mail.ntut.edu.tw

I.	Objective:
The purpose of this course is, first, to clarify key concepts in the understanding and interpretation of literary/cultural texts, and secondly, to demonstrate the ways in which important steps and maneuvers should be undertaken in order to compose a piece of promising academic writing. All the research and academic writing in this field follows MLA Style, which then becomes a focus of study and practice in this course.

II. Textbooks:  
(1) MLA HANDBOOK (9th edition, 2022) for MLA Style in academic writing
(2) The Little Seagull Handbook with Exercises (Norton 2021)
(3) Digitalized reading materials selected from our reference books 

III. Syllabus
1.Week One and Week Two:
(1) Introduction of MLA style for academic writing; preparation  and organization of class activities
(2) Reading of pieces of prose  to grasp the topic sentence, the main idea, the supporting details, the conclusion, and the right vocabulary. 
(3) Reading of a series of picture books by Leo Leonni to capture the main idea of each story and formulate its topic sentence

2. Week Three: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 1-21
Practicum of MLA style: (1) research question (2) main theme (3) outline (4) abstract (5) summary
3. Week Four: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 22-40
Practicum of MLA style: (1) argument and support (2) chapter arrangement (3) conclusion (4) citation and bibliography

The 1st task: the key words related to the structure of stories, including plot development, rising action, conflict, (anti)climax, and the turning point.
4. Week Five: MLA HANDBOOK, pp.41-61
(1) Three-act structure: Setup, conflict, and resolution
(2) The hero's journey: Departure, adventure, and return

The 2nd task: Forms of narrative
5. Week Six: MLA HANDBOOK, pp.62-81
(1) linear, nonlinear, and interactive
6. Week Seven: MLA HANDBOOK, pp.82-100
(2) foreshadow, flashback, and warped time/space 
 
The 3rd task: Modes of narration
7. Week Eight: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 101-20
(1) the first, second, and third person point of view
8. Week Nine: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 121-44
(2) omnipresent and limited point of view 

9. Week Nine (Midterm exam week)

The 4th task: Analyzing the narrator and main character
10. Week Ten: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 145-65
(1) reliable and unreliable narrators
11. Week Eleven: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 166-86
(2) tragic flaw, inner conflict, psyche, double
12. Week Twelve: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 187-207
(3) shadow, ego, superego

The 5th task: Interpreting Culture and ideology
13. Week Thirteen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 208-26
(1) Dominant, recondite, and residual culture; cultural text and cultural practice; cultural hegemony; colonization
14. Week Fourteen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 227-47
(2) class, race, gender, ethnicity, colonization, and ecological awareness

The 6th task: Writing on different genres of literature
15. Week Fifteen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 248-69
(1) novel and prose
16. Week Sixteen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 270-92
(2) poetry and drama

The 7th task: Writing on arts and multi-media texts
17. Week Seventeen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 293-302
(1) painting and sculpture
18. Week Eighteen: MLA HANDBOOK, pp. 303-46
(2) TV drama and film 

IV. Percentile distribution of final grades
1.Presentations in the class: 20%
2.Final written report: 10 %
3.Participation in classroom discussion and attendance: 70%

V. Selections from the following reference books:
(1) A Glossary of Literary Terms by M.H.Abrams
(2) Critical Terms for Literary Study, eds. by Frank Lentricchia and Thomas McLaughlin
(3) The Literature Student's Survival Kit by Ian Littlewood
(4) On Writing Well: The Classic Guide to Writing Nonfiction by William Zinsser
(6) A Short Guide to Writing about Art by Sylvan Barnet

課程對應SDGs指標: SDG2：消除飢餓（Zero Hunger）SDG3：良好健康和福祉（Good Health and Well-being）SDG4：優質教育（Quality Education）SDG5：性別平等（Gender Equality）SDG10：減少不平等（Reduced Inequalities）SDG11：永續城市與社區（Sustainable Cities and Communities）SDG13：氣候行動（Climate Action）SDG16：和平正義與有力的制度（Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions）SDG17：夥伴關係（Partnerships for the Goals）
課程是否導入AI: ● 使用生成式 AI 作為教學過程的輔助工具（Use generative AI tools as an aid to the teaching process）
remarks: We will set up a LINE group for communication.The students enrolled in this class will be invited to join this LINE group by mail. For online classes, we will use Google Meet or LINE Chatroom.
